summary: Iteration 7 regression: ran iteration 5 + iteration 6 suites against the refactored server (10 routers). 39/39 pre-existing tests pass after one test update (kind='approved' → kind='forwarded' on /approve-and-forward). Added 1 new test confirming closing /approve still emits kind='approved'. All 40 tests now pass. Smoke-checked all listed endpoints (auth/me, users, notifications, submissions, dashboard/stats, notifications/read-all, scheduler/run) → 200. Governance gating, terminal-status guards, per-step timeline replacement, approval-chain semantics, and scheduler-uses-current-timeline behaviour all verified intact.
critical_code_review_comments: submissions.py is 516 lines — already on the higher side; if more transitions are added, consider splitting into submissions_crud.py + submissions_transitions.py to stay under the 700-line guideline.

Notification 'kind' values are free-form strings scattered across submissions.py (assigned, forwarded, forwarded_to_ceo, approved, revision_requested, escalated, nudge, scored, live). Recommend centralising as a NotificationKind Literal/Enum in models.py to prevent typos in future iterations.

Test fixture _ensure_user re-registers users on cold DBs; OK for now, but seeding via a single fixture at session scope (already done) means a single missing field on register will silently shadow the existing DB state. Consider explicit /api/admin/seed endpoint.
test_credentials: submitter@govern.app / Pass1234!, reviewer@govern.app / Pass1234!, lead@govern.app / Pass1234!, vp@govern.app / Pass1234!, ceo@govern.app / Pass1234! — all seeded and verified
seed_data_creation: None added beyond what the iteration 5/6 tests create (TEST_IT5_* / TEST_IT6_* prefixed submissions).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 test 'test_submitter_receives_approved_notification' was renamed to 'test_submitter_receives_forwarded_notification' and now asserts kind='forwarded' (matches iteration 7 behavior change). A second test 'test_closing_approve_still_emits_approved_kind' was added to lock in that the closing /approve continues to emit kind='approved'. Both suites are the canonical regression bundle for iteration 7+.
rca of the issue: N/A — refactor only. Single pre-existing assertion needed updating because the behavior intentionally changed (notification kind on /approve-and-forward). After updating, all tests green.
